episodes.title: Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor’s First Meeting With Peter Mandelson Revealed | Dickie Arbiter
episodes.description:
“He was somebody who was once an arch republican, and all of a sudden he’s siding up to the royals.”

Dickie Arbiter comments on the Sunday Times investigation which has revealed that Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor and Peter Mandelson first met at a lunch organised by the NSPCC in 1999, months before they were photographed with Jeffrey Epstein in bathrobes at a mansion on Martha’s Vineyard.

Queen Elizabeth II’s former press secretary Dickie Arbiter was speaking to Carole Walker on Times Radio Drive. Listen live everyday 4-7pm.

episodes.title: Can the King Strip Beatrice and Eugenie of Their Titles?
episodes.description:
Questions are being raised about Princess Beatrice and Princess Eugenie’s royal titles amid renewed attention on the monarchy following the Epstein files and the arrest of their father, Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor.

#royalfamily #princessbeatrice #princesseugenie #princeandrew

Roya Nikkhah and Kate Mansey discuss the future of the York sisters within a slimmed-down monarchy, and whether the spotlight on their roles is unfair or an inevitable consequence.

Do you think Princess Beatrice and Princess Eugenie should keep their royal titles? Get in touch: theroyals@thetimes.co.uk

Image: Getty

Producer: Robert Wallace
Executive Producer: Priyanka Deladia

A discerning look at royal life in the era of King Charles, presented by the royal editors of The Times and Sunday Times, Kate Mansey and Roya Nikkhah – two women with unmatched insight into the inner workings of the monarchy. It’s a time of challenges and change for the royal family, but also one of great hope and warmth, and a sense of the possibilities of this new Carolean age. 

There are personal challenges for the royals, but new links are being forged with “kind words”, as King Charles has said, between the public and the Palace. Join Roya and Kate every week for their reactions to the latest news on Charles and Camilla, William and Kate, Harry and Meghan and more.

episodes.title: Queen Extends Sympathies to Epstein’s Victims After Andrew Fails To | Kate Mansey
episodes.description:
“This is her most deep intervention yet.”

Queen Camilla shows “strongest sign” yet” of extending sympathies to Epstein’s victims, says The Times’s royal editor Kate Mansey.

Kate Mansey was speaking to Kate McCann and Stig Abell on Times Radio Breakfast. Listen live every day 6-10am.

episodes.title: Andrew Scandal Could Reach ‘Very Close To The Throne’ | Andrew Lownie
episodes.description:
“This crisis is getting very close to the throne itself.”

Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor’s behaviour was enabled by a culture of protection within the royal household and the King must clarify “what he knew and when” as the scandal risks damaging the wider monarchy, says historian and author Andrew Lownie.

Andrew Lownie was speaking to Harry Wallop on Times Radio. Listen every Saturday 1pm-4pm.

episodes.title: Andrew Epstein Scandal: Police Ask For Time, But ‘They’ve Had 16 Years' | Matt Wilkinson
episodes.description:
"There seems to be more momentum now for the police to investigate."

We have had pictures of Epstein and Andrew since 2010, but there seems to be more “political will’ to investigate now - something which the police perhaps should have looked at many years ago, says The Sun’s royal editor, Matt Wilkinson.

Matt Wilkinson was speaking to Jane Garvey on Times Radio. Listen live from 2pm-4pm Monday to Thursday.

episodes.title: ‘The Royal Family Has Covered Up This Story For 15 Years’ | Andrew Lownie
episodes.description:
“It’s very clear that the Royal Family has covered up the story for 15 years.”

An inquiry into how much the Royal Family knew about the nature of Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor’s history with Jeffrey Epstein would be welcome after they’ve tried to “suppress” the story, claims the author of Entitled, Andrew Lownie.

episodes.title: Buckingham Palace furious Andrew won’t acknowledge victims | The Royals
episodes.description:
As the fallout from the ‘Andrew problem’ rolls on, William is in Brazil to present the Earthshot Prize and represent the King and government at the environment summit, COP30, as he attempts to steer the agenda towards green innovation. But can William also shift the royal narrative? And will the Andrew story require a bigger break from the past for the royals?

episodes.title: Andrew & Epstein - The King acts
episodes.description: He will now be known simply as Andrew Mountbatten Windsor. The man formerly known as Prince Andrew is being stripped of his titles and removed from his mansion, Royal Lodge, on the Windsor estate. Last month The Times obtained a copy of the leasehold agreement for Royal Lodge which showed that while the prince paid £1 million for the lease plus at least £7.5 million for refurbishments completed in 2005, he has paid “one peppercorn (if demanded)” in rent per year, since 2003. Andrew's links with convicted sex offender Jeffrey Epstein brought increasing public and political demands for action. It is a seismic moment in royal history – but is it enough? Roya is joined on the podcast by Aubrey Allegretti, the paper's chief political correspondent and George Greenwood, investigations reporter for The Times. Presenter: Roya Nikkhah Contributors: Aubrey Allegretti, Times chief political correspondent and George Greenwood, Investigations Reporter for The Times Producers: Sophie McNulty, Priyanka Deladia, Robert Wallace, Stephen Titherington Photo: Getty Images Hosted on Acast.
